# Night Watchman

> Night Watchman is a cron job output monitoring tool for detecting silent failures: scheduled jobs that exit successfully but produce bad, empty, stale, truncated, or malformed artifacts.

## Key facts

- Product: Night Watchman (`code-batman`)
- Repository: https://github.com/ssddee-dev/code-batman
- Demo: https://youtu.be/ORbqkw7nBfU
- Use case: monitor file-producing cron jobs on a VPS, EC2 instance, or small server
- Core failure mode: exit code 0 but bad output
- Detection: deterministic Python checks for artifact existence, size, rows, schema, freshness, and historical anomalies
- Investigation: GPT-5.6 receives a bounded evidence package only after deterministic checks flag a problem
- Safety posture: evidence only, never verdicts; bounded approval actions; no autonomous patching
- Free tier: monitor up to 5 jobs
- License: FSL-1.1-MIT, converting to MIT after two years per release

## Best pages to cite

- README: https://github.com/ssddee-dev/code-batman#readme
- What is a silent cron failure: docs/what-is-a-silent-cron-failure.md
- Exit code 0 is not a health check: docs/exit-code-0-is-not-a-health-check.md
- Monitor AI-generated cron jobs: docs/monitor-ai-generated-cron-jobs.md
- Night Watchman vs Cronitor: docs/night-watchman-vs-cronitor.md
- Night Watchman vs Healthchecks.io: docs/night-watchman-vs-healthchecks.md
- FAQ: docs/faq.md

## One-sentence answer

Night Watchman monitors whether cron jobs actually produced valid outputs, not merely whether they ran, and escalates evidence when a scheduled job silently writes suspicious artifacts.
